title: stints: PBP → lineup stint derivation (turn stints-contracts green)
description: Implement nba/stints/derive.py. derive_stints(pbp_events, starters_home, starters_away) -> list[Stint] with quarter, start_clock_s, end_clock_s, home_lineup (frozenset[int]), away_lineup, home_pts, away_pts, possessions, raw_event_ids. Algorithm: init from starters; iterate time-ordered PBP; sub closes+opens; quarter boundary closes+opens (carry forward if no fresh sub data, flag mismatches); Dean Oliver possessions. Pass brutus contracts unmodified. Follow-up bead for unanticipated real-data cases.

title: Postgres schema design (docs/schema.md + migrations/0001_init.sql)
description: Design Postgres schema. docs/schema.md + migrations/0001_init.sql (Postgres 16 + pgvector). Tables: players, teams, games, coaches, coach_games, rosters, pbp_events (players_on_floor jsonb), lineup_stints (home_lineup int[5], away_lineup int[5], pts, possessions), facts (subject_type enum, subject_id, fact_key, fact_value jsonb), sim_cache (lineup1_hash, lineup2_hash, context_hash, model_versions jsonb, result jsonb), embeddings_player (pgvector). Indices: stints(game_id,quarter,start_clock_seconds); pbp(game_id,quarter,clock_seconds); embeddings_player ivfflat cosine; sim_cache UNIQUE(lineup1_hash,lineup2_hash,context_hash); facts(subject_type,subject_id,fact_key). lineup_hash = SHA256 of sorted (player_id|season), hex.
notes: Closed at 9cc00b1 — docs/schema.md + migrations/0001_init.sql shipped. 11 tables, pgvector dim=128, lineup_hash spec locked.

Empty placeholder modules ok. Verify: pip install -e . works, pytest runs, ruff check ., pyright . pass.

title: cli: implement nba players similar / search / career (turn brutus contracts green)
description: Three new Typer subcommands under nba/cli/players (or wherever existing players show lives). (1) similar --id --k K: reads pgvector embeddings table, returns top-K by cosine distance. With random-init embeddings the result is effectively noise — fine for now; emit warning 'random_init_embeddings' so the UI can show its hint. (2) search --q QUERY: substring/ILIKE on players.name; returns (player_id, name, season) rows (one per (player,season) per spec — 'one embedding per (player, season)' from SPEC.md). (3) career --id ID: returns per-season stat lines from the rosters/facts tables; stub null stats are acceptable if facts table not yet populated (just emit warning 'facts_table_empty' on null fields). All three return the standard {data, warnings, meta} envelope per nba/contracts.py. Update nba/contracts.py with new pydantic models SimilarOutput / PlayersSearchOutput / PlayersCareerOutput. Done = brutus's contract tests pass; full envelope shape validated.

title: brutus: write contract tests for nba players similar / search / career (new subcommands)
description: The web GUI needs three new CLI subcommands. Write FAILING black-box pytest contract tests (mirror tests/test_cli_contract.py style) for: (1) nba players similar --id ID --k K → SimilarOutput envelope, data.neighbors = [{player_id, name, season, distance}] length<=K, sorted ascending distance, warnings[] non-fatal allowed. (2) nba players search --q QUERY → PlayersSearchOutput, data.results = [{player_id, name, season}] empty list allowed (no error), warnings[] may include 'no_matches'. (3) nba players career --id ID → PlayersCareerOutput, data = {player_id, name, seasons: [{season, team, games, mpg, ppg, rpg, apg}]} (stub values fine; stat lines may be missing → fallback nulls). InvalidPlayerError (exit 3) on unknown id for similar and career. EraOutOfRangeError NOT applicable to these. Hand-off CONTRACT.md addendum + bead update to cli-lane when red is captured.
notes: red captured at 6b54f78. 9/9 behavior tests fail in tests/test_cli_contract.py with 'No such command' from typer (exit 2). 1 smoke test (contract_models_importable) passes intentionally — models were added in nba/contracts.py so the implementer can import them and write green. CONTRACT.md addendum + .brutus/nba-v0n/CONTRACT.md + .brutus/nba-v0n/transcript.md landed in the same commit.

title: web: hero matchup card + replacement picker + sim renderer + warnings/stub pills
description: The home-page main interactive surface. Components: (1) MatchupCard — two team-year roster columns side by side; clicking a roster cell opens the picker; staged swaps render with the original struck-through above the replacement in green. (2) PlayerPicker (Radix Dialog) — single view, top has always-visible search input, body has 'Similar' list (top ~12 from nba players similar --id X --k 12), search filters via nba players search --q QUERY. Empty-states: 'no players matching X', 'random-init embeddings; pick from search instead' when similar tab returns nothing. (3) Simulate button — wires staged swaps into the team1/team2 specs as 'team-year[swap=A->B,C->D]', calls nba sim, renders skeleton while pending. (4) SimRenderer — score (mono), win prob with CI band, 5 matchups list with edge magnitude bar (green/red by sign) + cross-matchup-flag note, team_edges as 4 sign-driven bars (rebounding/halfcourt_fit/spacing/defensive_switchability), scouting_take prose block. (5) WarningPill (red, click-to-expand) for warnings[]; meta.model_versions containing stub/v0/placeholder triggers a tiny mono 'stub' pill. season_fallback pills go under the team header; sparse_data pill on the matchup card. (6) Reset link next to Simulate clears staged swaps. (7) Cache-hit gets a 'cached' badge. (8) On error: typed errors (InvalidPlayerError/InsufficientDataError/EraOutOfRangeError) render code as banner title; untyped fall back to 'sim failed; check terminal'; retry button re-fires same sim. First-visit default: Knicks 2024 vs Pacers 2024; subsequent visits restore from localStorage key 'nba.hero.matchup'. Done = full flow click-swap-simulate-render works against the real nba CLI.

title: Lane A: nba ingest season subcommand → DB
description: Wire nba/ingest/espn.py (already exists) to Postgres. Subcommand: nba ingest season --team NYK --season 2023 [--dry-run]. Populates teams/players/games/rosters/pbp_events/coach_games. Idempotent (re-running = no diffs). 30s rate-limit. Cache to data/cache/espn/. Bad-PBP: <50 plays → log warning, pbp_status='thin', keep boxscore, skip stints, don't crash. Smoke test: NYK 2022-23 → 82 games, ~35-40k pbp_events, 15-20 distinct rosters; gated by env var.
notes: Closed at e0e500a. nba/ingest/season.py + tests/test_ingest_smoke.py. D1/D2/D3 rulings applied (season=end-year; players_on_floor='[]'::jsonb sentinel; make db-up already loops migrations/*.sql). Smoke gates all green for NYK 2022-23: 82 games / 38534 pbp_events / 16 distinct NYK rosters / never-NULL placeholder / idempotent re-run. coach_games is team-of-interest side only (82 NYK-side rows; opponent-side is slice-3 scope). Mitigation: nullify pbp_events.player_id when it's a non-player participant (coaches in technical-foul plays — Mike Budenholzer was the first one to surface). 47/47 mine + 9/9 brutus + 5/5 smoke = green. CI gate via NBA_INGEST_SMOKE=1. docs/schema.md appended with D2 placeholder contract. nba/config.py exposes ESPN_CACHE_DIR for ml-lane.

title: cli: wire players similar/search/career to real DB tables (drop stub warnings)
description:
Follow-up to nba-g0s. The three new player subcommands currently return shape-valid stubs sourced from the hardcoded KNOWN_PLAYERS set in nba/cli/main.py, with documented warning codes (random_init_embeddings, no_matches, facts_table_empty) signaling that the underlying data isn't real yet. When the prereqs land, replace the stubs and drop the warnings:

(1) nba players similar — replace stub neighbor list with a pgvector cosine query against embeddings_player (players_embedding table per docs/schema.md). Drop random_init_embeddings warning once embeddings are trained (no longer random-init). Prereq: ml-lane real embeddings training output landed.

(2) nba players search — replace the KNOWN_PLAYERS substring loop with ILIKE on players.name from the real players table; return one row per (player, season) per SPEC.md (one embedding per (player, season)). Prereq: ingest-lane populated players + rosters.

(3) nba players career — replace the null-stats stub with a join against the facts table for per-season stat lines (games, mpg, ppg, rpg, apg). Drop facts_table_empty warning once facts has rows. Prereq: facts table populated (likely a separate ingest/derive lane).

Each subcommand should preserve the SAME envelope shape — only the data contents and warnings change. Black-box contract tests in tests/test_cli_contract.py already validate the shape and will continue to gate this work; new tests can be added to assert non-stub behavior (e.g. neighbors not equal to alphabetical roster slice; facts_table_empty warning absent when facts has rows). Also consider unifying _resolve_player and _resolve_player_id — both substring-match against KNOWN_PLAYERS; _resolve_player_id additionally strips a 'stub-' prefix. With real DB-backed resolution they collapse to one DB lookup helper.

title: web: Lineups page (5-slot picker + season + look-up + recent-list)
description: Top-tab nav: Lineups page. Five search slots (each opens a small player picker reusing PlayerPicker minus the Similar tab) + a season number input + Look-up button. Calls nba lineup stats --players ... --season Y. Renders result card with stint_count, possessions, net/off/def rating. Recently-queried lineups persist in localStorage key 'nba.recent.lineups' (last 8); shown in a side list under the form, clicking restores the slots. Pre-2003 → render the EraOutOfRangeError banner (typed). Sparse-data warning renders red pill on the result card with n_effective tooltip from warning.context.
notes:
--- web-pages-lane plan (2026-05-11) ---

OWNED FILES
- web/src/pages/Lineups.tsx
- web/src/components/LineupPicker/LineupPicker.tsx
- web/src/components/LineupPicker/Slot.tsx
- web/src/components/LineupPicker/Result.tsx
- web/src/components/LineupPicker/RecentList.tsx
- web/src/components/LineupPicker/index.ts

VERIFIED ENVELOPE (captured live 2026-05-11)
nba lineup stats --players a --players b --players c --players d --players e --season 2023
data: {stint_count:int, possessions:int, net_rating:float, off_rating:float, def_rating:float, players:string[5], season:int}
warnings: sparse_data {context:{n_effective}}
meta: {model_versions:{...stub...}, data_versions, cache_hit, cached, generated_at, stub}

COMPONENT TREE
Lineups (page) -> LineupPicker (top) + Result (below picker on lookup) + RecentList (right sidebar)
LineupPicker -> 5x Slot + season number input + Look-up button
Slot -> filled: name + ×; empty: "+" button -> opens web-core PlayerPicker (Search tab only)

PROPS / STATE
Lineups props: { prefill?: {players: string[5], season: number} } from BrowseRail
Lineups state: slots: (PlayerLite|null)[5], season:number (default 2024), result, loading, error
LineupPicker props: { slots, season, onSlotChange(i, player|null), onSeasonChange(n), onLookup() }
Slot props: { player: PlayerLite|null, onPick(player), onClear() }
Result props: { data: LineupStatsData, warnings, meta }
RecentList props: { items: RecentLineup[], onPick(item) }

EXACT CLI CALL (via lib/api.ts wrapper)
- lineupStats(playerIds[5], season) -> argv ["lineup","stats","--players",p1,"--players",p2,"--players",p3,"--players",p4,"--players",p5,"--season",String(season)]

LOCALSTORAGE
- nba.recent.lineups (last 8 FIFO, dedupe by sorted-players+season): extended schema
  stored: {players: string[5], season:number, net_rating:number}
  Shared-context canon names only {players[5],season}; adding net_rating is additive (non-breaking, render-only).
  BrowseRail and RecentList both read this.

ERROR / EMPTY STATES
- season < 2003 client-side guard before submit -> inline "season must be 2003 or later"
- EraOutOfRangeError exit 4 (server) -> typed banner "season before 2003 not supported"
- sparse_data warning on result -> red Warning pill on Result card, tooltip surfaces n_effective from context
- meta.model_versions stub/v0/placeholder -> StubPill on Result card
- meta.cached true -> small "cached" badge
- untyped error -> "lookup failed; check terminal"
- < 5 slots filled -> Look-up button disabled, hint "fill all 5 slots"

OPEN COORDINATION (blocking — route via athena before slot picker wiring)
web-core PlayerPicker must expose a way to hide its Similar tab. Suggested prop: hideSimilar?:boolean (or tabs?:('search'|'similar')[]). Until confirmed I will draft Slot to import PlayerPicker and call it with hideSimilar={true}; if web-core's API differs we adapt on signal.

GATING
Hold writes until athena signals scaffold ready. Until then: bead notes only.

title: web: Players page (search + detail with career + nearest neighbors + use-as-swap-target)
description: Top-tab nav surfaces a Players page. Search input → results list (calls nba players search --q QUERY). Click row → detail view: identity (name, seasons from nba players show), per-season stat line (nba players career --id ID), embedding-nearest neighbors (nba players similar --id ID --k 8). 'Use as swap target' button: stashes player into localStorage key 'nba.recent.swap-target' and navigates to home tab; home reads that and pre-stages a placeholder swap UI on the hero card (or simply highlights the picker open state). Recently-viewed players written to localStorage key 'nba.recent.players' (last 12). Empty states match the spec.
notes:
--- web-pages-lane plan (2026-05-11) ---

OWNED FILES
- web/src/pages/Players.tsx
- web/src/components/PlayerDetail/PlayerDetail.tsx
- web/src/components/PlayerDetail/Career.tsx
- web/src/components/PlayerDetail/Similar.tsx
- web/src/components/PlayerDetail/index.ts

COMPONENT TREE
Players (page) -> SearchBox + ResultsList (left) + PlayerDetail (right)
PlayerDetail -> Header + UseAsSwapTargetButton + Career + Similar

PROPS / STATE
Players props: { setTab(tab), openPlayerId?: string } (openPlayerId from BrowseRail click)
Players state: q (debounced 250ms), results, selectedId, loading, error
PlayerDetail props: { id, onPickNeighbor(id), setTab(tab) }
PlayerDetail state: career, similar, loading, error
Career props: { data: PlayersCareerData, warnings, meta }
Similar props: { neighbors: SimilarNeighbor[], warnings, onPick(id) }

EXACT CLI CALLS (via lib/api.ts wrappers — do NOT add new wrappers)
- playerSearch(q)  -> argv ["players","search","--q",q]                      data: {results:[{player_id,name,season}]}
- playerCareer(id) -> argv ["players","career","--id",id]                    data: {player_id,name,seasons:[{season,team,games,mpg,ppg,rpg,apg}]}
- playerSimilar(id, 8) -> argv ["players","similar","--id",id,"--k","8"]     data: {neighbors:[{player_id,name,season,distance}]}
Identity (name, player_id) comes from playerCareer response — do NOT call players show.

LOCALSTORAGE
- nba.recent.players (canonical, string[] last 12 FIFO, dedupe): push selectedId on row select.
- nba.recent.swap-target: write {player_id, name} on Use-as-Swap-Target click, then setTab('home').
- nba.players.namecache (additive, Record<id,{name,last_season}>): populate from career response for BrowseRail to render player cards without re-fetching. NOT in the shared-context canonical list but additive and shape-stable.

ERROR / EMPTY STATES
- q empty -> "search for a player to see career and neighbors"
- search 0 results -> "no matches for 'q'"
- InvalidPlayerError exit 3 on career/similar -> inline banner "player not found"
- random_init_embeddings warning on similar -> render shared-context hint "random-init embeddings; pick from search instead" inside Similar list, suppress the list rows.
- facts_table_empty warning on career -> "no per-season facts available yet" caption under empty table.
- meta.model_versions stub/v0/placeholder -> StubPill (from web-core) on detail header.
- meta.cached true -> "cached" badge on detail header.
- untyped error -> "lookup failed; check terminal"

GATING
Hold writes until athena signals scaffold ready. Until then: bead notes only.

OPEN COORDINATION
None blocking. lib/api.ts wrappers playerSearch/playerCareer/playerSimilar must exist; the shared context names them in the API-wrapper convention — assume web-core delivers them, escalate via athena if missing post-scaffold.

title: web: 'More matchups' rail with preset matchup cards
description: Horizontal-scroll snap row under the hero. Curated preset list at web/src/data/presets.ts (start with ~6: knicks-2024 vs pacers-2024, warriors-2016 vs cavaliers-2016, lakers-2010 vs celtics-2010, bulls-1996 vs sonics-1996, heat-2013 vs spurs-2013, knicks-2026 vs warriors-2016 [cross-era anchor from SPEC.md]). Each card lazily calls nba sim with results memo'd in module memory (no localStorage for sim payloads — spec disallows). Compact preview: matchup label + score + win-prob percentage. Clicking promotes that matchup to the hero slot, replacing the hero card's content and writing the new matchup id to localStorage.

title: Brutus: embeddings_player post-train smoke contract (Lane C)
description: POST-TRAIN smoke test for ml-lane's embeddings training. Asserts: embeddings_player has rows for the season (count = distinct players who played that season per rosters table), dim=128, vectors normalized to unit length (||v||≈1 within tolerance), season key = end-year int. Filed per D4 ruling — training is a script entrypoint (python -m nba.train.embeddings or scripts/), NOT a CLI subcommand. Brutus picks up when ml-lane is ready to implement nba-ibw.
notes:
red captured at b0eda9e. tests/test_embeddings_smoke.py — 5 errors against empty embeddings_player table (current state: 0 rows, NYK 2022-23 roster has 19 distinct players). Gated by NBA_EMBEDDINGS_SMOKE=1. transcript: .brutus/nba-bbq/transcript.md. nba/contracts.py untouched per D4. Full suite 170 passed + 11 skipped — no regression. Ready for ml-lane to train; smoke goes green when (1) row count == 19, (2) vector_dims==128, (3) ||v||≈1, (4) season=2023 on every row.
GREEN confirmed by brutus at 7bed00c. NBA_EMBEDDINGS_SMOKE=1 pytest tests/test_embeddings_smoke.py: 5 passed. 463 embedding rows, model_version=embeddings-v0-randinit, dim=128, L2-norm within 1e-7, 19 NYK 2022-23 roster players covered, season=2023 set. VERDICT.md at .brutus/nba-bbq/VERDICT.md. Closing.

title: Lane D: SFT corpus pipeline (Reddit + yt-dlp + Whisper) to disk
description: Output data/corpus/{reddit,transcripts}/*.jsonl with {source,url,text,metadata}. Quality filters: min karma, dedupe, optional profanity strip. NO LoRA training this run. Goal: get corpus to disk, wc -l it. Verify 5-10k LoRA-rule reachable before LM planning. Document corpus size + filter rules in docs/sft_corpus.md. Sample pipeline — not full historical pull.
notes: Sample pipeline shipped. 17,081 lines on disk (17,026 reddit + 55 transcripts). 5 subs × 50 top posts (min_karma=5, url+body dedupe, profanity off by default) + 3 Thinking Basketball videos (yt-dlp + faster-whisper tiny). PullPush backend live as of 2026-05-11. Verdict: 5-10k LoRA threshold REACHABLE — see docs/sft_corpus.md. NO LoRA training (slice 3). CLI subcommand wiring pending brutus contract — routed to athena.

title: Lane C: predictor MLP v0 + MLflow + real nba sim
description: Small MLP ~5M params. Inputs: concat(sum,mean) home + concat(sum,mean) away + era + home/away. Target: home_pts - away_pts per possession from lineup_stints. Adam, no FSDP, CPU OK. No RAPM in v0. MLflow tracking local-only: model_version, train loss, val MSE, n_stints. Stub meta.model_versions to read latest MLflow run. Replace stub in nba sim — same JSON, Hungarian-assigned matchups by embedding distance, synthesized team edges, scouting_take = placeholder string. Brutus's 14 sim tests stay green.
notes: GREEN. Implementation: nba/predictor/{model,version,split,data}.py, nba/train/predictor.py ('python -m nba.train.predictor --season 2023'), nba/sim/{constants,loader,matchup,edges,run}.py, edits to nba/cli/main.py sim() body. Target switched per stints-lane: y = pts/duration_seconds with weight=duration (sidesteps 5.5% Oliver-weight bias). Training: AdamW wd=0.1, 3 epochs (longer overfits to random-init noise); final train_loss=0.0011, val_mse=0.00093. MLflow file backend logs runs + weights artifact; manifest at data/models/predictor_latest.json. Canonical 'nba sim --team1 knicks-2024[swap=kat->randle,divincenzo] --team2 pacers-2024' returns score 114-116, win_prob 0.401, real player names (Jalen Brunson, Buddy Hield...), 5 matchups, 4 team_edges, scouting_take present (or null with --no-scouting), meta.stub=False, model_versions populated. 14/14 sim contract tests GREEN. 170-test suite GREEN. Ruff + pyright clean. Season fallback warning surfaces when requested year (2024) not in embeddings (we have 2023).

title: Lane D: typed-error audit + InvalidPlayerError on bad team1
description: nba sim --team1 'wat' throws raw ValueError. Should be InvalidPlayerError JSON on stderr, exit 3. Audit nba/cli/main.py + nba/cli/teamspec.py for unwrapped exceptions, route through typed-error formatter. Brutus contract test class: every parse error → typed-error JSON.
notes: green at d5acc0a: 19/21 nba-5ve tests passing. Teamspec parse errors now route through typed-error formatter (InvalidTeamError 6 / InvalidSeasonError 7). Sim swap-in players validated against KNOWN_PLAYERS → InvalidPlayerError 3. Slice-1 14/14 still green. test_teamspec 11/11 still green. Ruff/pyright clean. Remaining 2 fails (argv7 ingest, argv8 stints) blocked on nba-kve (Lane A) and nba-8oj (Lane B) — out of scope for this bead.

title: Lane C validation gate: held-out 5 NYK games
description: Pick 5 NYK 2022-23 games NOT in training set. Run nba sim with actual starting lineups. Compare predicted to actual scores. Won't be accurate — but predicted scores 90-130 per side (not 50 or 300). Document docs/predictor_v0_eval.md.
notes: GREEN. Validation gate PASSED — all 10 sides (5 games × 2) within [90, 130]. Range: 106-124. MAE: home=16.8 pts, away=13.0 pts, margin=15.0 pts, MSE/side=325.9. Holdout games (random.Random(20260511) on sorted NYK 2022-23 game_ids): [401468429 NY-POR, 401468705 NY-PHX, 401469092 NY-BKN, 401469159 LAC-NY, 401469174 LAL-NY]. Starters helper at nba/sim/starters.py: primary read = ESPN summary cache (config.ESPN_CACHE_DIR/summary/{game_id}.json + data/fixtures/espn/{season}/{game_id}.json fallback). Cache empty this run → DB fallback (first stint of lineup_stints) supplied all 10 sides. Eval driver scripts/eval_predictor_v0.py. Deliverable: docs/predictor_v0_eval.md. 170-test suite + ruff + pyright clean on Lane C files.

title: Lane C: embeddings (player,season) → pgvector embeddings_player
description: PyTorch from scratch. 128-dim vector per (player, season). Inputs: z-score per-season stats (pts/ast/reb/stl/blk/tov/fga/fta/3pa/min) from SQL view over pbp_events aggregations. Era token = per-season scalar. Random init OK for v0 (goal: wire pgvector end-to-end). Persist to embeddings_player.
notes: GREEN. Implementation: nba/embeddings/{__init__,version,model,persist}.py + nba/train/embeddings.py. Entrypoint: 'python -m nba.train.embeddings --season 2023' (the --team flag is informational; we embed all 463 rostered players in 2023 across all 30 teams). 463 rows persisted to embeddings_player with model_version=embeddings-v0-randinit, dim=128, L2-normalized (||v||-1 within 1e-7). MLflow run logged under mlruns/. Brutus's nba-bbq smoke 5/5 PASSED. 170 tests in suite green, ruff + pyright clean on new files. Decisions: (a) embedded league-wide not NYK-only so nba-dd1 has opponent vectors without retraining; brutus's NYK-cohort smoke is contract-compatible with superset. (b) skipped migrations/0003 view — random-init has no stat consumer in v0.

title: web: 'Browse' rail (recently-viewed players + lineups, heterogeneous)
description: Second horizontal-scroll rail on the home page (below 'More matchups'). Reads localStorage keys 'nba.recent.players' and 'nba.recent.lineups', interleaves cards (player cards show name+seasons; lineup cards show 5 player initials + season + net rating). Empty-state hint: 'browse from the Players or Lineups tab'. Clicking a player card jumps to Players tab w/ that player open; clicking a lineup card jumps to Lineups tab w/ slots prefilled.
notes:
--- web-pages-lane plan (2026-05-11) ---

OWNED FILES
- web/src/components/BrowseRail/BrowseRail.tsx
- web/src/components/BrowseRail/PlayerCard.tsx
- web/src/components/BrowseRail/LineupCard.tsx
- web/src/components/BrowseRail/EmptyHint.tsx
- web/src/components/BrowseRail/index.ts

COMPONENT TREE
BrowseRail -> horizontal-scroll row of interleaved PlayerCard | LineupCard | (EmptyHint when both empty)

PROPS / STATE
BrowseRail props: {
  setTab(tab: 'home'|'players'|'lineups'),
  setOpenPlayerId(id: string),
  setLineupPrefill(p: {players: string[5], season: number})
}
BrowseRail internal: reads via useLocalStorage hook from web-core
  players: string[]            from 'nba.recent.players'
  lineups: RecentLineup[]      from 'nba.recent.lineups'  ({players, season, net_rating})
  namecache: Record<string,{name,last_season}>  from 'nba.players.namecache'
PlayerCard props: { id, namecache, onClick(id) }
LineupCard props: { lineup: RecentLineup, namecache, onClick(lineup) }

INTERLEAVE
Both lists are FIFO ordered most-recent-first. Render zipper: take from whichever was touched more recently. Since localStorage entries don't carry timestamps, we keep a parallel timestamp store 'nba.recent.touch' as Record<key, ISO> updated each push in Players/Lineups. For v1, simpler: alternate (player, lineup, player, lineup, ...) skipping when one runs out. Pick the alternate approach — keep it stateless. Document tradeoff in PR description.

LOCALSTORAGE (read-only consumer)
- nba.recent.players (string[] last 12)
- nba.recent.lineups ({players,season,net_rating}[] last 8)
- nba.players.namecache (Record<id,{name,last_season}>)
Writes happen in Players.tsx and Lineups.tsx (owners of those keys).

CARD CONTENT
PlayerCard: name (from namecache[id].name or fallback to id) + small "S{last_season}" badge
LineupCard: 5 last-name tokens space-separated (from namecache; fall back to id) + season + net_rating (mono, green if >=0 red if <0)

EMPTY STATE
Both lists empty -> single hint card "browse from the Players or Lineups tab"

CLICK BEHAVIOR
PlayerCard onClick -> setOpenPlayerId(id); setTab('players')
LineupCard onClick -> setLineupPrefill({players, season}); setTab('lineups')

CSS / DENSITY
overflow-x-auto, snap-x, snap-mandatory; card 1px border #e5e5e5, 8px radius, hover bg #f5f5f5; mono numbers.

GATING
Hold writes until athena signals scaffold ready. Plan only.

OPEN COORDINATION
- BrowseRail mounted on Home — coordinate with web-core's nba-ast Home layout. Athena handoff: where does Home compose this rail vs MatchupsRail? Shared context says "below 'More matchups'" rail. Confirm Home composition pattern matches.
- useLocalStorage hook must support default values + same-tab updates (storage event won't fire same-tab); fall back to a small custom hook if web-core's doesn't.

title: Calibrate possessions estimator: 0.44→0.46 + EOP dead-ball
description: Lane B v2 follow-up surfaced by nba-9b2 validation: deriver's Oliver possessions estimate is ~5.5% systematically low vs basketball-reference team-level NYK 2022-23. Root cause: 0.44 FTA weight is an old league-avg constant; modern is ~0.46. Plus the formula doesn't book end-of-period dead-ball possessions. Net rating is bias-free (symmetric undercount) so Lane C is not blocked, but raw off/def per-100 are inflated by ~6pt absolute. Fix: change 0.44 to 0.46 in nba/stints/derive.py and add a small additive term for EOP dead balls; re-validate against team totals.

title: Lane D: --human formatter buildout per DESIGN_FICTION
description: Right now --human just prints JSON + thin header. Build to match DESIGN_FICTION.md output: score header, matchup table, team edges, scouting take, warnings section. Implementer choice on tiny rendering helper. No rich dep unless trivial.
notes: green at a26daac: --human formatter shipped. nba/cli/human.py renders score header (with (alt) marker on swap side), Hungarian-assigned matchup table, team edges, scouting take (auto-omitted on --no-scouting), and bulleted warnings (dedupes n_effective). 11 unit tests in tests/test_human.py pin the layout. Pure stdlib (textwrap), no rich dep. Slice-1 14/14 still green; typed-errors 20/21 (argv8 stints still blocked on Lane B nba-8oj); test_teamspec 11/11; test_human 11/11. Ruff + pyright clean.

value:
RANKED SOURCES — pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API + ESPN NBA endpoints + Python/R wrappers + auth/rate limits + PBP coverage. No synthesis.

== Repo (confirmed) ==
1. github.com/pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API — primary repo; "complete list of undocumented API endpoints, URL params, JSON formats across 20+ sports (NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL); live curl examples, routing guides, Django REST API service."
2. github.com/pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API/blob/main/espn_service/README.md — Django service exposes /api/v1/ingest/teams/, /api/v1/ingest/scoreboard/ ingestion endpoints wrapping ESPNClient.
3. github.com/pseudo-r/Public-ESPN-API/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md — ESPNClient methods: get_team_injuries, get_team_depth_chart, get_team_transactions, get_game_situation, get_game_predictor.
4. gist.github.com/akeaswaran/b48b02f1c94f873c6655e7129910fc3b — original community gist (predecessor); pseudo-r forked it in May 2023.

== ESPN endpoints (NBA-relevant) ==
5. zuplo.com/learning-center/espn-hidden-api-guide — base URLs: site.api.espn.com (rich pre-assembled) vs sports.core.api.espn.com (granular, $ref links). Query params: dates=YYYYMMDD, season, seasontype=1|2|3, week, limit, groups=50.
6. scrapecreators.com/blog/espn-api-free-sports-data — concrete NBA example using 2016 Finals Game 7; no auth keys, plain GET → JSON.
7. sportsapis.dev/espn-api — 2026-dated overview; "Free Access, No Authentication, JSON Responses."
8. apify.com/crawlerbros/espn-rosters-player-stats — "ESPN's public API has no bot protection for typical use."

== Rate limits / auth ==
9. gist.github.com/akeaswaran/.../comment-3991891 — tsearle7: "no official rate limit since the API isn't official either. Other developers suggest 30 sec - 1 min when automating."
10. espnapi.com/espn-api-official-or-unofficial — confirms unofficial status; no stated SLAs; behind CDN; data may shift without notice.

== Historical PBP coverage ==
11. espn.com/nba/story/_/id/8951483/nba-adds-all-historical-statistics-website — ESPN added every box score back to 1946-47, but PBP is much shallower in practice.
12. hoopr.sportsdataverse.org/reference/espn_nba_game_all.html — hoopR R pkg pulls ESPN PBP via game_id (e.g., 401283399); play tables include sequence_number, text, away_score, home_score, scoring_play, score_value.
13. cran.rstudio.com/web/packages/hoopR/hoopR.pdf — hoopR CRAN manual; family includes espn_nba_betting, espn_nba_game_all, espn_nba_game_rosters.
14. nbastuffer.com/analytics101/playbyplay-data — overview of NBA PBP availability across providers; lineup data per game/season CSVs.

== Python/R prior art for stint reconstruction ==
15. github.com/swar/nba_api — Python wrapper to stats.nba.com (NOT ESPN); active (v1.11.4 Feb 2026); PlayByPlayV2 + many endpoints; no auth required.
16. github.com/swar/nba_api/blob/master/docs/examples/PlayByPlay.ipynb — official PBP notebook example.
17. pypi.org/project/nba_api — current package.
18. github.com/dblackrun/pbpstats — "scrape and parse NBA/WNBA/G-League PBP. Adds lineup on floor for all events; possession start/end, score margin, prev possession end" — most relevant for stint logic.
19. pbpstats.readthedocs.io — docs; data_provider options: stats_nba | data_nba | live; StatsNbaPossessionLoader builds Possession objects.
20. github.com/abresler/nbastatR — R wrapper, NBA Stats / BBR / RealGM; supports parallel; last updated 2021 (stale).
21. nbainrstats.netlify.app/post/adding-lineups-to-nba-play-by-play-data — walks adding lineups to PBP using nbastatR.
22. github.com/shufinskiy/nba_data — bulk historical NBA PBP dumps from stats.nba.com, data.nba.com, pbpstats.com, shots since 1996-97.
23. github.com/mcbarlowe/nba_parser — Python: parses PBP dataframes into player/lineup/team stats, pairs with nba_scraper.

== Contested / Unclear ==
- "Rate limits": no source cites a hard number from ESPN; community heuristic is 30s-60s between calls. Some scrapers run faster with no reports of bans.
- ESPN PBP historical depth: box scores go to 1946-47 but PBP detail varies — hoopR uses event IDs reliably for modern seasons (2002+ is the common practical floor across NBA stats sources, not explicitly stated for ESPN).

== Couldn't find ==
- Explicit ESPN-side statement of PBP earliest year / coverage SLA.
- Whether site.api.espn.com NBA endpoints are CDN-cached uniformly or per-region.

RANKED SOURCES — RAPM lineage, Bayesian shrinkage, lineup models, embeddings, Hungarian assignment. No synthesis.

== Foundational RAPM / APM ==
1. supermariogiacomazzo.github.io/STOR538_WEBSITE/Articles/Basketball/Basketball_Sill.pdf — Sill (2010) "Improved NBA Adjusted +/- Using Regularization and Out-of-Sample Testing" — original RAPM paper, MIT Sloan; ridge / Tikhonov regularization on APM; cites Rosenbaum 2004 and Hoerl 1962.
2. Rosenbaum, D.T. (2004) "Measuring How NBA Players Help Their Teams Win" — referenced everywhere as the APM origin (link via 82games.com historically); pure regression on stints.
3. arxiv.org/pdf/1008.0705 — Macdonald (2010) "On Estimating the Ability of NBA Players" — extends APM/RAPM; offensive/defensive split.
4. dunksandthrees.com/about/epm — EPM lineage page: clean explanation of APM → RAPM (Sill 2010) → modern hybrid metrics; "Sill reported nearly doubling APM accuracy."
5. nbastuffer.com/analytics101/nba-plus-minus — survey of plus-minus family: Roland Rating, APM (Winston/Sagarin), SPM (Rosenbaum), RAPM (Sill), RPM/xRAPM (Ilardi/Engelmann).
6. squared2020.com/2017/09/18/deep-dive-on-regularized-adjusted-plus-minus-i-introductory-example — accessible derivation showing λ=0 → OLS APM, λ=∞ → zeros; ridge as dimensionality reduction.
7. godismyjudgeok.com/.../a-review-of-adjusted-plusminus-and-stabilization — review tying APM/RAPM stabilization, year-over-year reliability.

== Bayesian RAPM / shrinkage / informed priors ==
8. arxiv.org/abs/2601.15000 — "Lineup Regularized Adjusted Plus-Minus (L-RAPM): Basketball Lineup Ratings with Informed Priors" — 2026 paper on lineup-level RAPM with priors.
9. jeremiasengelmann.substack.com/p/nba-adjusted-plus-minus-how-to-build — Engelmann (xRAPM author): "If multicollinearity is high for a pair like Gobert and Nash they end up with almost the same rating using standard RAPM. By introducing Bayesian priors — distinct starting values for each player's offensive and defensive ratings — we can better disentangle their impacts."
10. stat.cmu.edu/~brian/nynke/726-2021/.../nbaproject_9422_6496778_Final%20Presentation.pdf — CMU "Bayesian Contract Plus-Minus (BCPM)" — uses contract values as priors on ridge regression.
11. pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12671482 — "Bayesian two-stage framework for lineup-independent assessment of individual rebounding ability" — modern two-stage Bayesian approach building on Sill 2010.
12. blog.evanmiya.com/p/bayesian-performance-rating — Bayesian Performance Rating (BPR) for CBB; 4-year RAPM training dataset for shrinkage param tuning.
13. apbr.org/metrics/viewtopic.php?t=9844 — APBRmetrics forum thread "Incorporating Prior Information into RAPM" (DSMok1, TopDownHockey 2020); practitioner-level methods.
14. jarad.me/research/papers/Niem_eval_2010.pdf — Niemi (2010) "Evaluating Individual Player Contributions in Basketball" — Bayesian framework for APM-style estimation.
15. bookdown.org/staedler_n/highdimstats/ridge-regression-and-shrinkage.html — ridge as Bayesian MAP with Gaussian prior; textbook treatment.

== Per-possession lineup models in literature ==
16. degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/jqas-2022-0120 — "Estimating positional plus-minus in the NBA" JQAS 2022 — positional decomposition of plus-minus.
17. cdn.prod.website-files.com/.../Estimating%20Positional%20Plus-Minus%20in%20the%20NBA.docx.pdf — same paper PDF; offensive/defensive split per position.
18. workshop.sportvis.com/papers/plump.pdf — "Expanding Plus-Minus for Visual and Statistical Analysis of NBA Box Scores."
19. www.ramapo.edu/dmc/wp-content/uploads/sites/361/2023/05/MSDS-Byman.pdf — Byman MS thesis: statistical learning model for evaluating NBA players via RAPM.
20. annualreviews.org/doi/pdf/10.1146/annurev-statistics-040720-015536 — Annual Review of Statistics 2021 "Basketball Analytics" survey — covers possession-level models broadly.
21. dept.aueb.gr/sites/default/files/stat/diplomatikes/pdf/Damoulaki.pdf — AUEB MSc thesis: regularized RAPM models, offensive/defensive top-50.
22. sloansportsconference.com/research-papers/nba-lineup-analysis-on-clustered-player-tendencies — Kalman (Purdue) clustered player tendencies → lineup efficiency.

== Player embeddings ==
23. arxiv.org/abs/2302.13386 — "NBA2Vec: Dense feature representations of NBA players" — word2vec-style player embeddings, 2018 NBA Hackathon.
24. sloansportsconference.com/research-papers/using-deep-learning-to-understand-patterns-of-player-movement-in-the-nba — unsupervised 32-dim trajectory embeddings from movement data.
25. cs.toronto.edu/~zemel/documents/1536-Classifying-NBA-Offensive-Plays-Using-Neural-Networks.pdf — embeddings resolve player positions in plays.
26. github.com/shrivasshankar/nba-clustering — autoencoder embeddings on 1.4M player-season rows for archetypes.
27. squared2020.com/2018/10/04/understanding-trends-in-the-nba-how-nnmf-works — NNMF for player/team trend factorization.
28. reference-global.com/download/article/10.2478/ijcss-2025-0005.pdf — "Analysing Basketball Shots with Graph Embedding."
29. sloansportsconference.com/research-papers/possession-sketches-mapping-nba-strategies — ML method organizing possessions by offensive structure into a sketch dictionary.

== Hungarian assignment ==
30. en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hungarian_algorithm — Kuhn 1955 origin; O(n³) primal-dual.
31. cp-algorithms.com/graph/hungarian-algorithm.html — standard formulations of assignment problem.
32. web.eecs.umich.edu/~pettie/matching/Kuhn-hungarian-assignment.pdf — Kuhn original paper.
33. andrea-grianti.medium.com/the-hungarian-algorithm-a-use-case-for-football-managers — Hungarian for picking player→role assignments in football (closest applied analogue to basketball matchup).
34. towardsdatascience.com/optimum-assignment-and-the-hungarian-algorithm-8b1027628028 — practitioner explanation.
35. medium.com/@ryancmiele/optimizing-defensive-matchups-using-machine-learning-02e23447a8dc — Bayesian Optimization for defensive matchup (not Hungarian, but related applied problem).
36. thespax.com/nba/using-individual-matchup-data-to-evaluate-defense-in-the-nba — uses ESPN/NBA individual matchup tracking data to score defense.

== Contested / Unclear ==
- Whether contract-value priors (CMU BCPM) help vs hurt: Engelmann favors box-score-derived priors; CMU project shows contract-value priors are viable; no head-to-head benchmark cited.
- Hungarian in basketball: no first-class academic paper applying Hungarian assignment specifically to NBA matchup attribution found — only sport-adjacent (football lineup) and tangential ML matchup-optimization work.

== Couldn't find ==
- A canonical academic paper using Hungarian assignment to reconstruct NBA defensive matchups from PBP/tracking data (the closest is heuristic spatial tracking, not formal Kuhn-Munkres).
- Direct comparison of stint-level vs possession-level RAPM in a single paper.
